disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Small-volume bright-red-blood-and-mucus stools (not cupfuls of dark blood/clots); no postural syncope; haemodynamically stable by proxy
trigger_question_examples: Is it small streaks/amounts of blood and mucus, or cupfuls of dark blood and clots?
Do you feel faint or nearly black out when you stand up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Small amounts of bright red blood and mucus each time, not cupfuls or clots, and I don't feel faint standing up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever/rigors and no severe constant generalised abdominal pain/rigidity (against perirectal abscess/toxic megacolon/infective colitis)
trigger_question_examples: Any fever or shakes, or a severely painful tender lump near the anus?
Is your tummy soft, or hard and severely painful all over?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever or shakes, no painful lump near the anus, and my tummy is soft, not rigid.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Tenesmus with small-volume rectal bleeding after lapsed topical mesalazine in known an inflammatory bowel condition of the rectum
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a diagnosed bowel condition, and did you run out of your suppositories?
Is this like your usual rectal inflammation flares?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I have an inflammatory bowel condition of the rectum and ran out of my suppositories two weeks ago; this is like my flares but worse.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Am I bleeding out? Do I need hospital tonight?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain that small-volume bright-red-blood-and-mucus with stable posture (no syncope) is a rectal inflammation flare, not a major bleed; restart the topical mesalazine, use heat for cramps, and give a major-bleed/sepsis safety-net — without ED over-triage, but escalate for large-volume bleeding/postural syncope/fever.
